description:
Advances: mutagen.cli, mutagen.json_schema

Closes **F7** (HIGH, Security reviewer H2). `--json <path>` accepts any binary; no canonicalisation. Combined with the in-process compile-and-execute design (Security C1), this is an arbitrary-file-write primitive: a malicious mutated test could write the report to `/etc/`, `~/.ssh/`, or a symlinked path.

## Sites
- lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex:206-217 (parse)
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:626-641 (write)

## Deliverable

- Canonicalize the path: refuse `..` traversal segments, reject NUL bytes, follow symlinks ONLY if target stays inside project root.
- Default policy: writes must land inside the projects root (resolved via `File.cwd!/0` or `Mix.Project.deps_path/0` parent).
- Escape hatch: `--unsafe-json-outside-project` flag explicitly opts in to writing outside the project root (CI use cases). When set, log a warning at startup.
- Revise `.spec/specs/cli.spec.md` + `.spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md`: new requirement for path safety.

## Verification

- New cli_test.exs cases: path with `..`, NUL byte, symlink to /etc, symlink staying inside project — assert each is accepted/rejected per the policy
- mix test green
- Spec verification stubs pass

## Allowed touches

- lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex (write site)
- .spec/specs/cli.spec.md, json_schema.spec.md
- test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs
- README.md, CHANGELOG.md

## Out of Scope

- Sandbox the entire mutation run (out-of-process workers) — that is Wont-Have / v2
- Refactor JSON write path beyond the canonicalization

## Merge gate
- [ ] mix compile --warnings-as-errors / mix test green
- [ ] Symlink traversal test passes
- [ ] Spec verification passes
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE

IMPLEMENTED
Session: 866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0.jsonl

Stage worktree: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-json-path-canon
Stage branch: mutagen-wrd.21/json-path-canon (branched from epic/mutagen-wrd @ b2b8800; epic has since advanced to 332c8c9 — orchestrator owns rebase/fast-forward)
Commit: a5e4d4b09fa8b9eaa3875b8c3613249d34cfb419

Files touched:
- .spec/specs/cli.spec.md (added r10, scenarios s10a–s10e, verification stub v5)
- .spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md (added "unsafe_json_path" to r5 abort_reason vocabulary)
- lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex (validate --json literal at parse; new --unsafe-json-outside-project flag; new :unsafe_json_path reason atom)
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex (new phase_json_path hook before phase_scope; moduledoc updated; new Caveat)
- lib/mutagen_ex/config.ex (new unsafe_json_outside_project field, default false)
- lib/mutagen_ex/json_path.ex (NEW — single home for path-safety contract: validate_literal/1 + canonicalize/2)
- lib/mutagen_ex/json_reporter.ex (added :unsafe_json_path to abort_reason type list)
- test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs (12 new tests: r10/s10a/s10b literal checks, --unsafe-json-outside-project flag wiring, parse-time abort-JSON path)
- test/mutagen_ex/json_path_test.exs (NEW — 19 tests covering validate_literal/1, canonicalize/2 inside-root check, symlink-escape rejection, symlink-stays-inside-root acceptance, escape-hatch flag, project-root resolution)
- test/mutagen_ex/json_canonicalisation_test.exs (NEW — 8 integration tests driving phase_json_path through Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.run/2 for s10c/s10d/s10e)
- README.md (--json safety section + new flag in flags table)
- CHANGELOG.md (Security entry + Added entries for JsonPath module and the new flag)

Tests added:
- MutagenEx.JsonPathTest — 19 tests
- MutagenEx.JsonCanonicalisationTest — 8 tests
- MutagenEx.CLITest — 12 new tests (parse-time --json safety, --unsafe-json-outside-project wiring, parse-time abort-JSON dispatch)

Verification stubs flipped: mutagen.cli.v5 (new) — execute: true. Existing v1, v2, v4 remain execute: true.

Audit-expanded scope: none (this is iteration 1).

Deferred must requirements: none. Every "must" element of the ticket Verification block is covered:
- "path with `..`" → MutagenEx.JsonPathTest "rejects a path containing a `..` segment" + MutagenEx.CLITest "path with `..` is rejected at parse time as :unsafe_json_path (s10a)"
- "NUL byte" → MutagenEx.JsonPathTest "rejects a path containing a NUL byte (s10b)" + MutagenEx.CLITest "path with embedded NUL byte is rejected at parse time (s10b)"
- "symlink to /etc" → MutagenEx.JsonPathTest "rejects a symlink whose target escapes the project root (s10c)" + MutagenEx.JsonCanonicalisationTest "symlink whose target escapes the project root aborts before any mutation phase"
- "symlink staying inside project" → MutagenEx.JsonPathTest "accepts a symlink whose target stays inside the project root (s10d)" + MutagenEx.JsonCanonicalisationTest "symlink resolution succeeds; canonical path lands on Config"
- mix test green → 397 tests, 0 failures (was 362; +35 new tests)
- Spec verification stubs pass → v1, v2, v4, v5 all green

Summary: Implemented two-layer path-safety contract for --json <path>. Parse-time check (MutagenEx.JsonPath.validate_literal/1) refuses NUL bytes and `..` segments; filesystem canonicalisation (MutagenEx.JsonPath.canonicalize/2) runs in a new phase_json_path hook before any mutation phase, resolving symlinks and refusing paths whose fully-resolved target escapes the project root. --unsafe-json-outside-project is the documented opt-in for CI artifact paths and emits a one-shot stderr warning naming the resolved target. New spec invariant mutagen.cli.r10 with scenarios s10a–s10e; mutagen.json_schema.r5 extended with "unsafe_json_path" abort reason.

Note for orchestrator: stage branch was created off b2b8800; epic has advanced to 332c8c9 (mutagen-wrd.28). Rebase or fast-forward strategy is owned by the orchestrator before merge-back.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 12
- Files outside Allowed globs: 5
  - lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex                            -> Allowed
  - lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex                         -> Allowed
  - .spec/specs/cli.spec.md                          -> Allowed
  - .spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md                  -> Allowed
  - test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs                     -> Allowed
  - README.md                                        -> Allowed
  - CHANGELOG.md                                     -> Allowed
  - lib/mutagen_ex/json_path.ex (NEW)                -> Outside Allowed (implementer-claimed structural)
  - lib/mutagen_ex/config.ex                         -> Outside Allowed (implementer-claimed structural; +1 field)
  - lib/mutagen_ex/json_reporter.ex                  -> Outside Allowed (implementer-claimed structural; +1 atom in @type)
  - test/mutagen_ex/json_path_test.exs (NEW)         -> Outside Allowed (implementer-claimed structural)
  - test/mutagen_ex/json_canonicalisation_test.exs (NEW) -> Outside Allowed (implementer-claimed structural)
- Files matching Out-of-Scope (files) globs: 0
  - "Sandbox the entire mutation run": NO MATCH. json_path.ex validates and canonicalises a single Config field; it does not spawn workers, restrict mutation IO, or isolate phases. No process-level sandbox primitive introduced.
  - "Refactor JSON write path beyond canonicalization": NO MATCH. lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex default_io/3 and the File.write!/IO.write call sites are byte-identical pre/post (git diff shows zero edits inside default_io/3 or any emit_* function). The only upstream change is that Config.json_path now holds the canonical form instead of the user literal.

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 4 executable (cli.v1, cli.v2, cli.v4, cli.v5 [NEW]), passed: 4. cli.v3 remains execute:false (unchanged).
  - mix test test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs -> 45/0 (v1)
  - mix test test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs --only exit_codes -> 6/0 (v2)
  - mix help mutagen -> rendered moduledoc with required sections + path-safety caveat (v4)
  - mix test test/mutagen_ex/json_path_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs -> 64/0 (v5 NEW; covers r10)
- Requirements coverage (one line per touched must requirement):
  - mutagen.cli.r10: covered by test/mutagen_ex/json_path_test.exs and test/mutagen_ex/json_canonicalisation_test.exs. Falsifiability per claim:
    * ".." traversal rejected -> json_path_test.exs:52-73 (variant: :traversal asserted on three shapes: leading "../..", middle "out/..", absolute "/tmp/.."); cli_test.exs:200-228. Test would fail with {:ok, _} if validate_literal/1 dropped the segment-equality check.
    * NUL byte rejected -> json_path_test.exs:43-50 + 218-230; cli_test.exs:230-245. Asserts variant == :nul_byte. Would fail with {:ok, _} if String.contains?(path, <<0>>) check were removed; the escape-hatch test also asserts NUL refusal survives unsafe_outside_project: true.
    * Symlinks followed only when target stays inside project root -> json_path_test.exs:145-163 (rejects symlink -> /etc/hosts) + 183-195 (rejects parent symlink -> /tmp) + 165-181 (accepts symlink whose target stays inside root). Would fail if resolve_existing_prefix/2 stopped following symlinks (inside-root case would return the link path rather than target) or if inside?/2 short-circuited prematurely (outside-target symlink would slip through).
    * --unsafe-json-outside-project opts out with stderr warning -> json_path_test.exs:206-215 (accepts outside path with flag); json_canonicalisation_test.exs:184-220 (asserts stderr contains "warning", "--unsafe-json-outside-project", AND the resolved path). Companion test at 222-250 asserts NO warning when flag absent. Would fail if the IO.puts(:stderr, ...) line were dropped or the warning text omitted any of the three required substrings.
    * macOS /var -> /private/var canonicalisation -> json_path_test.exs:129-143 (asserts details.resolved == resolve_symlinks("/tmp/nope.json")) + 233-260 ("resolves a symlinked project root through symlinks"). The test helper resolve_symlinks/1 walks lstat itself, so on macOS it produces /private/tmp and the production code must match. Would fail with a literal == mismatch if resolve_root/1 short-circuited or used Path.expand only.
  - mutagen.json_schema.r5: extended vocabulary now includes "unsafe_json_path". Reporter @type abort_reason additionally lists :unsafe_json_path. cli_test.exs:535-566 asserts decoded["abort_reason"] == "unsafe_json_path" round-trips through :json.decode on a parse-time abort path. Would fail if emit_error/2 dropped Atom.to_string or if the new atom were not threaded through phase_cli/abort emission.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. The "sandbox" and "refactor write path" out-of-scope items both hold (see mechanical block above). The new code is purely a path-string and filesystem-resolver gate seated between phase_cli and phase_scope; mutation execution semantics are unchanged.
- Project verification: PASS — mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean; mix test = 397 tests, 0 failures (13 excluded e2e_slow).
- Audit-expanded scope (per step 11): none. No prior AUDIT: BOUNCE comments on this ticket; this is iteration 1.

Source-claim tracing (step 10):
- (1) ".." traversal rejected: CONFIRMED. lib/mutagen_ex/json_path.ex:184-188 has_traversal_segment?/1 splits Path.split(path) and Enum.any?(&(&1 == "..")). The "..foo / foo..bar / foo/...hidden" acceptance test (json_path_test.exs:75-82) is a negative-control that pins the segment-equality semantics — without segment splitting, those names would be falsely rejected.
- (2) NUL bytes rejected: CONFIRMED. lib/mutagen_ex/json_path.ex:84-90 (validate_literal) AND :139-145 (canonicalize defensive re-check). Both are tested; the escape-hatch test explicitly asserts NUL refusal still fires with unsafe_outside_project: true (json_path_test.exs:218-230).
- (3) Symlinks followed only when target stays inside project root: CONFIRMED. lib/mutagen_ex/json_path.ex:278-352 do_resolve/5 walks each existing component, re-resolving via File.read_link. The inside-root decision is taken once at the END against the fully-resolved path (line 161 inside?/2), not per-segment — this is necessary to accept the macOS case where /var -> /private/var is a legitimate intermediate symlink. Falsified by both the accept-symlink-inside test and the reject-symlink-escape test passing in tandem.
- (4) --unsafe-json-outside-project opt-out + stderr warning: CONFIRMED. lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:357-363 emits IO.puts(:stderr, ...) only when canonicalize/2 returned {:ok, _} AND config.unsafe_json_outside_project is true. The warning string interpolates the resolved canonical path. Tests at json_canonicalisation_test.exs:217-219 assert three independent substrings ("warning", "--unsafe-json-outside-project", and the resolved path) — each a falsifiable axis.
- (5) macOS /var -> /private/var: CONFIRMED. lib/mutagen_ex/json_path.ex:199-267 resolve_root/1 + do_resolve_root/4 walk the project_root itself through File.read_link before the inside-root comparison. The test helper resolve_symlinks/1 (json_path_test.exs:275-312) mirrors this algorithm and predicts the canonical absolute path; assertions compare ==, so a host where /tmp is a symlink to /private/tmp exercises the round-trip end to end.

Notes (scope-expansion judgment — implementer-claimed structural):
- lib/mutagen_ex/json_path.ex (NEW, 360 lines): STRUCTURALLY DEFENSIBLE but not strictly required. The contract (validate_literal/1 + canonicalize/2) could have lived as private functions inside lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex (which IS in Allowed). The split into a named module is a cleanliness choice that gives the path-safety contract a single home and a unit-test seam. CLI.parse/1 calling into MutagenEx.JsonPath.validate_literal at parse time would have required private-function reach from cli.ex into mutagen.ex (cross-module), so the module IS justified if you want CLI parse-time validation. The alternative (duplicate the NUL/`..` regex in cli.ex) is uglier. Net: justifiable but not the only path; orchestrator may treat as scope-expansion-with-cause.
- lib/mutagen_ex/config.ex (+1 field, +1 type entry, +1 docstring para): STRUCTURALLY REQUIRED in practice. The mix task phase_json_path/2 reads config.unsafe_json_outside_project at line 352. Without the field, the boolean would have to be threaded as a separate parameter or recovered from argv inside the phase — both are uglier than the single field. Config is documented in cli.spec surface (line 32) as part of the CLI subject, and r10 explicitly states "Config.unsafe_json_outside_project is true iff the flag was passed". Closing r10 without this field would either fail r10 or require an awkward workaround. RECOMMEND treat as legitimate scope-expansion.
- lib/mutagen_ex/json_reporter.ex (+1 atom in @type abort_reason): NOT STRICTLY RUNTIME-REQUIRED but DOC-CONSISTENT. emit_error/2 guards on is_atom(abort_reason) at line 162, so passing :unsafe_json_path at runtime works without the typespec change. However, mutagen.json_schema.r5 (which IS in Allowed via .spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md) now lists "unsafe_json_path" in its vocabulary; dropping the typespec update would create a contract drift between the spec text and the type. RECOMMEND treat as legitimate scope-expansion: the spec-text update inside the Allowed surface implies a one-line type update for type-spec parity. Not pure-required but defensible.
- test/mutagen_ex/json_path_test.exs (NEW, 23 tests) + test/mutagen_ex/json_canonicalisation_test.exs (NEW, 11 tests): NOT STRUCTURALLY REQUIRED. Both files COULD have lived inside test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs (which IS Allowed) — that file already has a "Mix.Tasks.Mutagen" describe block, and cli_test.exs:524-567 already runs parse-time s10a there. The split is opportunistic clarity (unit vs integration). The ticket Verification block names "cli_test.exs cases" as the verification site, so strictly the new test files are scope-expansion. They do not introduce coverage that could not have lived in cli_test.exs. RECOMMEND orchestrator allow this scope-expansion on cleanliness grounds; it does not enlarge the change surface materially.

Gaps: none. Every "must" element of r10 has at least one test with a falsifiable assertion. Project verification clean.

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: f4d09e2a-3b7c-4e91-8a52-1c0e0f8d7a4b
Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex-json-path-canon/f4d09e2a-3b7c-4e91-8a52-1c0e0f8d7a4b.jsonl

Reasoning: Every must element of cli.r10 has a falsifiable test (s10a..s10e independently asserted across json_path_test.exs, json_canonicalisation_test.exs, and the cli_test.exs additions); the verifier's source-claim tracing is accurate against the diff (json_path.ex:184-188 segment-equality, 161 inside?/2-at-end, 357-359 inside? string prefix). Mechanical re-check confirms 12 files touched / 5 outside Allowed / 0 matching Out-of-Scope file globs. Intent-level Out of Scope holds: no worker sandbox introduced (no processes spawned, no IO restriction), and default_io/3 + every emit_* call site in lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex is byte-identical pre/post — the only write-path change is that Config.json_path now carries the canonical form. Full suite green (397/0, 13 e2e_slow excluded); mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean.

Audit-expanded scope authorized retroactively for all 5 out-of-Allowed files:

1. lib/mutagen_ex/config.ex (+unsafe_json_outside_project field + typespec): STRUCTURALLY REQUIRED. r10 names this field by FQN as the boolean carrier ("Config.unsafe_json_outside_project is true iff the flag was passed"). Closing r10 without this field would either fail r10 or require an awkward parallel parameter through phase_json_path/2. Authorized.

2. lib/mutagen_ex/json_reporter.ex (+:unsafe_json_path in @type abort_reason): TYPESPEC PARITY with the r5 vocabulary update inside Allowed (.spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md). emit_error/2 guards on is_atom/1 so runtime works without the typespec change, but dropping it creates spec-vs-type drift on a contract the Allowed surface explicitly extended. Authorized as a one-line consistency edit downstream of an Allowed spec change.

3. lib/mutagen_ex/json_path.ex (NEW, 360 lines): ARCHITECTURALLY DEFENSIBLE. CLI parse-time validation requires a callable seam visible to both lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex and lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex. The alternatives — (a) private fns in mutagen.ex that cli.ex reaches into via cross-module private call, or (b) duplicated regex/segment logic in both files — are both worse than a named module. The contract (validate_literal/1 + canonicalize/2) is cohesive and unit-testable in isolation. Authorized as legitimate refactor pulled in by the security contract.

4. test/mutagen_ex/json_path_test.exs (NEW, 23 tests) + test/mutagen_ex/json_canonicalisation_test.exs (NEW, 11 tests): OPPORTUNISTIC CLARITY. Both files COULD have lived inside test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs (Allowed). The split is unit-vs-integration for the new JsonPath module and isolates the FS-touching phase_json_path tests behind their own process-dictionary fixture. Authorized on cleanliness grounds: (a) no enlargement of the change surface or behavioural risk, (b) the FS-touching integration tests have a meaningfully different fixture profile (Process.put + tmp-dir isolation + symlink planting) than the parse-time tests in cli_test.exs, (c) the unit-vs-integration split mirrors the module split, (d) verifier recommended allow and source-claim tracing is detailed enough to make a bounce-for-cleanliness a poor use of the bounce budget (2 total).

Bounce budget remaining: 2/2 (no bounces consumed). Stage branch a5e4d4b09fa8b9eaa3875b8c3613249d34cfb419 cleared for merge-back into epic/mutagen-wrd. Orchestrator still owns the b2b8800 → 332c8c9 rebase/fast-forward strategy noted by the implementer.
APPROVED. Merged into epic/mutagen-wrd. Closing per orchestrator sign-off (auditor APPROVE on iteration 1 with audit-expanded scope authorized retroactively).

title: Canonicalize --json <path> — reject path traversal + symlink escape
